SEARCH
Package org.javalabs.jpa

Class JdbcReader

java.lang.Object
org.javalabs.jpa.JdbcReader
public class JdbcReader extends Object
JDBC Reader object. This object is used to fetch data from the database. Every native entity manager will have it's dedicated reader object. Reader is not thread safe, however, since entity manager can not be shared across multiple threads, thus making this class implicitly thread safe. Reader object can support two kind of queries: 1. Simple SQL query 2. Complex query (using SQL type).
Author:
Sudiptasish Chanda

  • Method Details

    • getEm

      public LiteEntityManager getEm()
      Return the underlying entity manager, this writer is associated with.
      Returns:
      LiteEntityManager

    • get

      public <T> T get(Class<T> entityClass, Object primaryKey, jakarta.persistence.LockModeType lockMode, Map<String,Object> properties) throws JdbcException
      Fetch the object from the underlying store. Search for an entity of the specified class and primary key and lock it with respect to the specified lock type. JPA-LiTE will never search the persistent context, this the entity will be always be obtained from the DB If the lock mode type is pessimistic and the entity instance is found but cannot be locked: - the PessimisticLockException will be thrown. If the database locking failure causes transaction-level rollback. - the LockTimeoutException will be thrown. If the database locking failure causes only statement-level rollback If a vendor-specific property or hint is not recognized, it is silently ignored.
      Type Parameters:
      T -
      Parameters:
      entityClass - Java Type (class) of the entity.
      primaryKey - Primary key of the record.
      lockMode - Lock type
      properties - Vendor specific properties
      Returns:
      T Record matching with the primary key specified.
      Throws:
      JdbcException

    • fetchRelationship

      private Object fetchRelationship(Object parent, RelAttribute rel) throws SQLException
      Fetch the child records from the database. One entity may have OneToOne or OneToMany relationships with other entities from a different table. Often it is required the fetch such child record(s) once we are fetching the parent entity (record). That way the returned entity will be complete.
      Parameters:
      rel - Relationship type. E.g., OneToOne, OneToMany, etc
      Returns:
      Object
      Throws:
      SQLException

    • refresh

      public void refresh(Object obj, jakarta.persistence.LockModeType lockMode, Map<String,Object> properties)
      Refresh the state of this entity with that from the underlying database.
      Parameters:
      obj - Entity whose state to be updated.
      lockMode - Locking mode
      properties - Additional vendor properties

    • close

      public void close()
      Close this reader.
